coredata.dc:title: Synergistic effect of inner filtering effect and host-guest interaction based on β-cyclodextrin fluorophore for promoted 4-nitrophenol detection
coredata.prism:publicationName: Colloids and Surfaces A: Physicochemical and Engineering Aspects
coredata.prism:copyright: © 2024 Elsevier B.V. All rights are reserved, including those for text and data mining, AI training, and similar technologies.
coredata.prism:publisher: Elsevier B.V. All rights are reserved, including those for text and data mining, AI training, and similar technologies.
coredata.dc:creator: Zhang, Zhaolei
Fang, Fengyan
Zhang, Pingping
Zhang, Xiaokang
Ma, Hongchao
Wei, Yanhui
coredata.dc:description: Efficient detection of 4- nitrophenol (4-NP) organic pollutant is always a very important issue due to the rapid development of chemical industry and the abuse of pesticides and so on. Here, we designed and synthesized a novel supramolecular fluorescent probe, 4-[4-(acetylamino)styryl]-1-cyclodextrin pyridinium p-toluenesulfonate (ASPT-β-CD), for the selective and sensitive detection of 4-NP. The introduction of β-CD not only improves the water solubility of probe and can also be used as a host to encapsulate 4-NP. In alkaline aqueous solution, the absorption spectrum of 4-NP overlaps the excitation spectrum of ASPT-β-CD and suggests that an internal filtration effect occurs between them. We explored the possible response mechanism and speculated that the high selectivity and high sensitivity were derived from the synergistic effect of inner filtering effect and host-guest interaction. Benefiting from the synergistic effect, the proposed sensor ASPT-β-CD displayed significant "turn-off" after adding 4-NP, while showing a fast response to 4-NP within 2 min and low LOD about 0.01 μM. In addition, this method has been successfully applied to the determination of 4-NP in lake water samples, indicating that the obtained ASPT-β-CD has great practical application potential in the environmental field.
coredata.dcterms:subject: 4-nitrophenol
Cyclodextrin
Inner filter effect
Host-guest inclusion

xocs:meta.xocs:srctitle: Colloids and Surfaces A: Physicochemical and Engineering Aspects

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Synthesis and characterization of fluorescent ASPT-β-CD
Condition exploration and optimization of ASPT-β-CD toward 4-NP detection
Fluorescence sensing of 4-NP based on ASPT-β-CD supramolecular fluorescent probe

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Schematic illustration of the synergistic effect of IFE and host-guest interaction based on β-cyclodextrin fluorophore ASPT-β-CD for 4-NP detection.

Explore the excitation wavelength and pH conditions of ASPT-β-CD probe. (A) Fluorescence spectra of ASPT-β-CD in water at different excitation wavelengths (350–450 nm). (B) Dependence between fluorescence intensity and excitation wavelength. (C) Fluorescence spectra of ASPT-β-CD at different pH conditions. (D) Dependence between fluorescence intensity and pH values of ASPT-β-CD and ASPT.

Inner filtering effect and host-guest interaction of ASPT-β-CD toward 4-NP. (A) UV–vis absorption spectra and the corresponding photographs (B) of 4-NP aqueous solution with different pH conditions (Configure buffers for different pH: Adjusting the Britton-Robinson (BR) buffer solution to different pH values by adding 0.2 mol/L NaOH solution). (C) The degree of overlap between absorption spectrum of 4-NP (2-NP, 3-NP) and the excitation spectrum of ASPT-β-CD under pH = 8. (D) Fluorescence emission spectra of ASPT-β-CD in the presence of 4-NP. (E) FT-IR spectra of 4-NP, ASPT-β-CD, and 4-NP@ASPT-β-CD. (F) X-ray diffraction patterns of 4-NP, ASPT-β-CD and 4-NP@ ASPT-β-CD.

Condition exploration and optimization of ASPT-β-CD toward 4-NP detection. (A) Effect of polarity of different solvents (methanol, DMF and water) on the UV absorption of ASPT-β-CD. (B) Effect of time on the fluorescence intensity of ASPT-β-CD. (C) Effect of 2-NP, 3-NP and 4-NP on the fluorescence intensity of ASPT-β-CD at different pH values. (D) Variation of fluorescence intensity of 4-NP@ASPT-β-CD with time.

Fluorescence sensing of 4-NP based on ASPT-β-CD supramolecular fluorescent probe. (A) Synergistic effect for 4-NP detection by ASPT-β-CD probe. (B)Fluorescence emission spectra of ASPT-β-CD (1 mM) in the presence of different concentrations of 4-NP. (C) Linear relationship between the relative fluorescence values and the concentration of 4-NP. (D) Effect of possible interferents on the fluorescence quenching (condition: 1 mM ASPT-β-CD solution, 10 μM 4-NP and 10 μM possible interferents, pH = 8). (E) Reversibility of the as-developed ASPT-β-CD fluorescence nanoswitch mediated by pH. The pH of solution was mediated by alternately adding 0. 2 M NaOH and 0.2 M HCl to the mixture of ASPT-β-CD (1 mM) and 4-NP (10 μM).
Analytical results of 4-NP in Nai River water samples by our proposed method (n = 3).

head.ce:title: Synergistic effect of inner filtering effect and host-guest interaction based on β-cyclodextrin fluorophore for promoted 4-nitrophenol detection

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Chemistry and Material Science, Shandong Agricultural University, Taian, Shandong 271018, PR China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: College of Chemistry and Material Science, Shandong Agricultural University

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: College of Chemistry and Material Science, Shandong Agricultural University, Taian, 271018, Shandong, PR China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: State Key Laboratory of Heavy Oil Processing and Center for Bioengineering and Biotechnology, College of Chemical Engineering, China University of Petroleum (East China), Qingdao 266580, PR China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: State Key Laboratory of Heavy Oil Processing and Center for Bioengineering and Biotechnology, College of Chemical Engineering, China University of Petroleum (East China)

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: State Key Laboratory of Heavy Oil Processing and Center for Bioengineering and Biotechnology, College of Chemical Engineering, China University of Petroleum (East China), Qingdao, 266580, PR China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Efficient detection of 4- nitrophenol (4-NP) organic pollutant is always a very important issue due to the rapid development of chemical industry and the abuse of pesticides and so on. Here, we designed and synthesized a novel supramolecular fluorescent probe, 4-[4-(acetylamino)styryl]-1-cyclodextrin pyridinium p-toluenesulfonate (ASPT-β-CD), for the selective and sensitive detection of 4-NP. The introduction of β-CD not only improves the water solubility of probe and can also be used as a host to encapsulate 4-NP. In alkaline aqueous solution, the absorption spectrum of 4-NP overlaps the excitation spectrum of ASPT-β-CD and suggests that an internal filtration effect occurs between them. We explored the possible response mechanism and speculated that the high selectivity and high sensitivity were derived from the synergistic effect of inner filtering effect and host-guest interaction. Benefiting from the synergistic effect, the proposed sensor ASPT-β-CD displayed significant "turn-off" after adding 4-NP, while showing a fast response to 4-NP within 2 min and low LOD about 0.01 μM. In addition, this method has been successfully applied to the determination of 4-NP in lake water samples, indicating that the obtained ASPT-β-CD has great practical application potential in the environmental field.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Efficient detection of 4- nitrophenol organic pollutant is always a very important issue in chemical and environmental fields. In this work, we designed and synthesized a novel supramolecular fluorescent probe, 4-[4-(acetylamino)styryl]-1-cyclodextrin pyridinium p-toluenesulfonate (ASPT-β-CD), for the selective and sensitive detection of 4-NP through synergistic effect of inner filtering effect and host-guest interaction. Benefiting from the synergistic effect, the proposed sensor ASPT-β-CD displayed significant "turn-off" after adding 4-NP, while showing a fast response to 4-NP within 2 min and low LOD about 0.01 μM, having great practical application potential in the environmental field.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: Aromatic nitro-compounds are important chemical intermediates that are widely utilized for the production of pesticides, medicines, dyes and plastics. But these compounds often exhibit toxic effect on organism, which would result in irreversible damage not only to humans but also to animals, plants and some hydrobios[1–4]. Moreover, high solubility and stability of aromatic nitro-compounds in water will also pose a significant threat to water pollution. One such compounds is 4-nitrophenol (4-NP) which has been listed as typical hazardous chemicals by the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency[5,6]. Even a trace level consumption of 4-NP would cause serious health hazards such as liver, kidney damages, headache, methemoglobinemia, and so on.[7]. According to U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, the allowable limit of 4-NP in drinking water was set at no more than 0.43 μM. Considering its highly toxic effects, therefore, it is of great significance to establish a rapid, simple, highly selective and sensitive method for the detection of 4-NP.

So far, researchers have explored a variety of analytical methods to detect contaminants in water samples, such as capillary electrophoresis[8], electrochemistry[9–14], spectrophotometry[15,16], high performance liquid chromatography (HPLC)[15,17,18]. Unfortunately, these methods generally require large-scale and expensive instruments, complicated pretreatment, and professional operation[19–21] In order to improve its portability and operability, fluorescence method was developed and gradually widely applied in rapid detection of food quality and trace detection of contaminants[22] for the advantages of simple operation, fast reaction, low cost, and high sensitivity.Up to now, diverse fluorescent probes based on quantum dots[23–25], nanoparticles[26,27], metal-organic frameworks (MOFs)[28,29] etc., have been developed for small molecule analyte assays. Generally, analyte binding must influence the molecular and/or electronic structure of the fluorescence probe to induce the optical properties changes that can be detected by fluorescence instruments or visual colorimetry. The mechanism of fluorescence sensor mainly includes photoinduced electron transfer (PET), intramolecular charge transfer (ICT), aggregation induced emission (AIE), inner filter effect (IFE), förster resonance energy transfer (FRET) and the number of approaches is still expanding.

IFE refers to the phenomenon that the absorber absorbs the excitation light or emission light (or both) of the fluorophore, resulting in a decrease in the fluorescence intensity of the fluorophore[30,31]. Together with FRET, they are often used to guide the construction of florescent sensing platforms for 4-NP with high sensitivity, good selectivity[32,33]. In contrast, IFE-based induced florescence quenching is simpler and more flexible in the construction of sensors because it does not require any linking or specific action distance between absorber and fluorophore[30]. Therefore, IFE process is more widely used in the field of environmental detection. As one kind of electron-deficient aromatic compound, 4-NP is a good electron-acceptor substrate with strong absorption in the ultraviolet band in aqueous solution, but a very weak luminophores. This is beneficial for 4-NP to undergo the IFE process through absorbing the excitation or emission light of the fluorophore probe in the ultraviolet band, resulting in a decrease in the optical intensity of the probe without additional background fluorescence interference. An effective overlap between the absorption spectrum of the absorber (4-NP) and the excitation or emission spectrum of the fluorophore (fluorescent probe) can maximize the sensitivity of 4-NP detection[22,32,34]. Hence, the design and development of IFE based fluorescent sensor with appropriate spectrum are still challenging and important.

In this work, a novel supramolecular fluorescent probe β-CD modified 4-[4-(acetylamino)styryl]-1-cyclodextrin pyridinium p-toluenesulfonate (ASPT-β-CD) was designed and synthesized for selective and sensitive detection of 4-NP. The modification of β-CD endows the hemicyanine fluorophore with good water solubility and inclusion properties for 4-NP, which are beneficial for the detection of 4-NP in aquatic environments by host-guest interaction. More interestingly, for the well overlap of excitation wavelength of ASPT-β-CD and absorption of 4-NP, the IFE effect occurs between ASPT-β-CD and 4-NP, which makes the green fluorescence of ASPT-β-CD largely quenched after adding 4-NP under alkaline condition, and successfully realizes the rapid, sensitive and portable fluorescence detection of 4-NP.

Mono-6-O-(p-toluenesulfonyl)-β-cyclodextrin, 4-methylpyridine, 4-nitrophenol, 4-acetamidobenzaldehyde, o-nitrophenol (2-NP), m-nitrophenol (3-NP), p-aminophenol (4-AP), catechol (CC), resorcinol (RC), hydroquinone (HQ), o-phenylenediamine (OPD), m-phenylenediamine (MPD), p-phenylenediamine (PPD), phenyltriol (PHL), p-chlorophenol (4-CP), p-bromophenol (4-BP), p-fluorophenol (4-FP), phenol, N,N-dimethylformamide (DMF), and n-methylpiperidine were purchased from Aladdin Ltd. (Shanghai, China). Acetone were purchased from Tianjin Kaitong Chemical Reagent Co. Deionized water from Hangzhou Wahaha Group Co.

Firstly, mono-6-O-(p-toluenesulfonyl)-β-cyclodextrin (2.56 g, 1.9 mmol) and 4-methylpyridine (12 mL, 0.123 mol) were mixed and stirred at 85 °C for 12 h. Then the whole reaction solution was cooled to room temperature. After that, the product was precipitated after adding a large amount of acetone. The raw product was further purified by recrystallization. The product yield was about 80%.

Initially, 2.76 g (0.002 mol) 4-methyl-1-cyclodextrin pyridine p-toluenesulfonate and 0.39 g acetylamino benzaldehyde (0.0024 mol) were first dissolved in 20 mL DMF. Then an appropriate amount of N-methyl piperidine was added as to the mixture, which was then heated to 75 °C and reacted with stirring for 3 h. After cooling to room temperature, a large amount of acetone was added to the reaction solution to precipitate the product. The obtained yellow solid was re-crystallized in methanol to get the final product. The final product yield was about 70%.

The UV–vis spectra of the samples were recorded using UV-2700 Spectrophotometer. Fluorescence spectra were obtained by Cary Eclipse spectrometer. Infrared spectra were recorded using Nicolet iS50 FT-IR. The 1H NMR spectrum was measured on a Bruker Avance spectrometer using DMSO-d6 as solvent. The pH values of the samples were determined with an PHB-3 digital pH meter. X-ray diffraction (XRD) data were obtained on a Smartlab SE X-ray diffractometer.

The detection of 4-NP was performed by measuring the fluorescence emission of ASPT-β-CD in the presence of 4-NP with different concentrations of. Initially, 1.0 mL ASPT-β-CD aqueous solution (1.00 mM) was mixed with 1.0 mL aqueous solution of 4-NP. Then 8.0 mL PBS buffer (pH = 8) was added to the above solution. After incubation for 5 min, the fluorescence emission spectra were collected for further analysis.

The homologues of p-nitrophenol or the substances that are easy to interfere with the detection of p-nitrophenol in actual samples were selected as the test objects. Therefore, aqueous solution of 4-NP, 2-NP, 3-NP, 4-AP, CC, RC, HQ, OPD, MPD, PPD, PHL, 4-CP, 4-BP, 4-FP and phenol were prepared. After mixing the interfering substance with ASPT-β-CD solution (1.00 mM) and buffer solution was added. The fluorescence emission spectra were determined under the above determination conditions.

In order to study the possibility of practical application of the fluorescent probe ASPT-β-CD (1.00 mM), actual water samples were collected from river water. The actual water sample was first filtered by microporous membrane to remove large particulate matter. Then, standard 4-NP solution with different concentrations were added to the river water. Finally, 1 mL ASPT-β-CD (1.00 mM) aqueous solution was mixed with 1 mL of the above sample solution, which was then diluted to 10 mL with PBS buffer (pH = 8) and shaken for 1 min to record the fluorescence spectra.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Synthesis and characterization of fluorescent ASPT-β-CD
ce:section.ce:para: Since the fluorophore and absorber are the two main units of the IFE sensing system, the optical properties and band overlap of the two directly affect the quenching efficiency of IFE, thus indirectly affecting the detection sensitivity. Therefore, continuously exploring new nanomaterials and exploring suitable absorber-fluorophore combinations can effectively enhance the detection effect of the IFE sensing system.

In our work, IFE can occur effectively require the absorption spectrum of the 4-NP well overlaps with the excitation or emission spectrum of the fluorophore. Because the absorption spectrum of 4-NP located in the range of 250–450 nm, it is the primary problem to find or design a fluorescent probe with excitation spectrum or emission spectrum in this band. In order to successfully realize the detection of 4-NP through IFE, one effective strategy is to adjust the excitation light of the fluorophore sensor to this band to maximize the overlap with the 4-NP absorption spectrum. Hemicyanine dye is one kind of ICT dyes with a donor-π-acceptor (D-π-A) structure, according to our previous works, the excitation wavelengths of hemicyanine dyes can be adjusted through molecular tailoring and modification to redistribute the push-pull structure electron cloud [35,36]. The study found that modifying the amide bond on the skeleton of hemicyanine dye can get the green dye (ASPT) with high fluorescence emission intensity in water [35]. The important thing is that its excitation spectrum can overlap with the absorption spectrum of 4-NP.

In order to improve the water solubility of the probe to meet the detection requirements of 4-NP. In this work, we modified this fluorescent group to connect with cyclodextrin to construct a novel supramolecular fluorescent probe, named as ASPT-β-CD ( Fig. 1A, S1, S2). After modification, the water solubility is significantly improved because of the abundant hydroxyl groups in β-cyclodextrin[37,38]. By characterizing the absorption and emission spectra of ASPT-β-CD in water, it was found that ASPT-β-CD has larger stokes shift and stronger green fluorescence compare to the dyes unmodified β-CD, meanwhile it has a significant excitation band between 300 nm and 450 nm, still can overlap with the absorption spectrum of 4-NP (Fig. S3).

The use of fluorescent probes for detection is often achieved by using changes in the fluorescence signal caused by the addition of the analyte. The intensity-varying fluorescent probe detects the object species according to the change of fluorescence intensity. Since the fluorescence intensity is also affected by other factors such as excitation light and probe environment, hence, optical stability in these aspects is important for quantitative detection of object species[39]. Therefore, how to avoid the interference of these environmental factors on the fluorescence signal is very important in the detection process. Up to now, the methods including linking fluorescence to matrix, host-guest inclusion and doping can be used to improve the structural stability of the fluorescent probe and the stability of the fluorescence signal, thus greatly improving the accuracy and sensitivity of the detection[40–43]. β-CD is a supramolecular macrocyclic structure, the inclusion in cyclodextrins exerts a profound effect on the physicochemical properties of guest molecules, which is favorable for the stability of the dyes against the degradative effects of oxidation, visible or UV light and heat. In our work, the introduction of β-CD not only enhances the solubility of dyes, but also enables the ASPT-β-CD fluorophore to obtain good optical properties and excellent pH stability and chemical stability[44]. As shown in Fig. 2A, the fluorescence peak (position and shape) did not change significantly with the shift of excitation wavelength. But it displays significant intensity- excitation wavelength dependency relationship. When excitation wavelength was 380 nm, ASPT-β-CD showed the maximum fluorescence intensity at 516 nm (Fig. 2B). Therefore, 380 nm is chosen as the optimal excitation wavelength of ASPT-β-CD probe.

Due to the push-pull electronic structure, the fluorescence signals of ICT dyes are easily affected by environmental factors such as pH, polarity, viscosity, etc.,[45,46] so sometimes they are used as probes to detect changes in environmental parameters. Here, using ICT dyes as probes to detect analytes, the influence of environmental factors must be considered to reduce interference as much as possible. In a specific water detection environment, pH is an environmental factor that must be considered. Surprisingly, the fluorescence intensity of ASPT-β-CD was slightly fluctuated by the change of pH value (Fig. 2C，2D), which indicated that ASPT-β-CD has good pH stability. In order to clarify this unexpected phenomenon, the fluorescence behavior of β-CD-unmodified ASPT dyes with the same concentration at different pH was studied. As shown in Fig S4 and Fig. 2D, the fluorescence intensity of ASPT dye increased mainly as the increase of pH values from 2 to 9, and the fluorescence signal decreased obviously in pH>9 alkaline environment. The decrease of fluorescence at high pH may be due to the hydrolysis of the amide bond of the dye under strong alkaline conditions. Structurally, the only difference between the two is whether the β-CD is modified. Therefore, it is reasonable speculate that β-CD plays an important role in resisting pH interference. According to our previous research, a β-CD modified trans-4-[4-(dimethylamino)styryl]-1-methylpyridinium (DASP-β-CD) dyes, which have a similar structure with ASPT-β-CD, are easy to form intermolecular inclusion complexes[36]. Fig. 2D shows the fluorescence intensity of the ASPT-β-CD is stronger than that of the ASPT with same concentration, which is caused by the host-guest inclusion reducing the non-radiative transition. So good pH stability of ASPT-β-CD should may be come from the intermolecular inclusion.[37] The dye part is encapsulated in the cyclodextrin cavity, which greatly reduces the effect of acid and alkali on the push-pull structure of the dyes. The good pH stability of ASPT-β-CD also provides conditions for the flexible selection of pH in the subsequent 4-NP detection environment.

Highly efficient IFE process needs a high degree of overlap between the two spectra (absorption spectrum of 4-NP and the excitation spectrum of ASPT-β-CD). The excitation spectrum position of ASPT-β-CD is not affected by pH, while the absorption spectrum of 4-NP is highly dependent on pH. Since 4-NP is a kind of organic weak acid with a pKa value of approximately 7.1 [19,34], the dissociation is depended on the pH of aqueous solution. Under acidic conditions, the 4-NP exists in the form of molecule, whose absorption peak is located at 320 nm. And under alkaline conditions, its phenolic hydroxyl group is neutralized by OH- to form the oxygen anion of 4-NP, which increases the conjugated degree and leads to the red shift of absorption peak to 400 nm ( Fig. 3A) [34,47]. With the pH increases, 4-NP gradually changed from acidic structure to basic structure, along with a remarkable color change from colorless to yellow can be observed by the naked eye (Fig. 3B). This pH-controlled population distribution in acidic and basic structures puts forward requirements for the detection environment. As shown in Fig. 3C, there is a high overlap with the UV–vis absorption of 4-NP and the fluorescence excitation spectrum of ASPT-β-CD in pH = 8, meanwhile, there is no overlap between the absorption spectrum of 4-NP and fluorescence emission spectrum of the ASPT-β-CD, which suggests that the IFE but not FRET is the main quenching mechanism in this fluorescence quenching progress. That because FRET requires the emission spectrum of the fluorophore ASPT-β-CD (donor) has a large overlap with the absorption spectrum of the quencher 4-NP (acceptor)[32,33,48–50]. In addition, two isomers 2-NP and 3-NP have little overlap with the excitation spectrum of ASPT-β-CD in pH = 8, suggesting that IFE may be an important way to distinguish 2-NP, 3-NP and improve selectivity of 4-NP.

It is worth mentioning that the fluorescence intensity decreased to one tenth of the original (Fig. 3D). We speculate that the host-guest effect between β-CD and 4-NP may be another reason for the improvement of sensitivity. Refer to the systematic study from Takao Kokugan's research group, the binding constant for β-CD /4-NP complex is reported as 90.1 M−1, which indicating that the 4-NP tends to enter the cavity of cyclodextrin[51].

In order to verify the formation of 4-NP@ASPT-β-CD inclusion complex, FTIR and X-ray diffraction were used to characterise the host-guest interaction between 4-NP and ASPT-β-CD. Fig. 3E, F show the FTIR and X-ray diffraction characterisation of 4-NP, ASPT-β-CD and 4-NP@ASPT-β-CD inclusion complexes (which was prepared by fully grinding 4-NP and ASPT-β-CD in a 1:1 ratio). Fig. 3E shows the FTIR spectra of 4-NP, ASPT-β-CD and 4-NP@ASPT-β-CD inclusion complexes. The bands at 1658 cm−1, 1596 cm−1 are characteristic peaks of CC stretching vibration and benzene ring skeleton vibration, respectively. The intensity of the peaks (1658 cm−1, 1596 cm−1) of 4-NP@ASPT-β-CD is significantly lower than that of the corresponding peaks of ASPT-β-CD. In addition, the C-N stretching vibration at 852 cm−1 and the symmetric stretching vibration of -NO2 at 1345 cm−1 from 4-NP are also significantly reduced in 4-NP@ASPT-β-CD, and meanwhile the characteristic peaks -NO2 located at 1345 cm−1 in 4-NP moves to 1338 cm−1 in 4-NP@ASPT-β-CD. The intensity reduction and position shift both indicate that the cyclodextrin cavity of ASPT-β-CD encapsulated 4-NP successfully. This conclusion was further confirmed by X-ray diffraction patterns characterization. As shown in Fig. 3F, the patterns of 4-NP are almost masked by the host molecule ASPT-β-CD, meantime, patterns of ASPT-β-CD are almost unchanged after encapsulating 4-NP. Pure ASPT-β-CD prefer to form intermolecular inclusion complex and generate supramolecular aggregates. Due to the large steric hindrance of the dye ASPT-β-CD and the presence of functional groups such as cyclodextrin cavities, hydroxyl groups, benzene rings, pyridine cations, etc. in the structure, there are multiple intermolecular forces, including host-guest interactions, hydrogen bonding, π-π interactions and electrostatic forces, resulting in irregular assembly.[52] Therefore, the ASPT-β-CD powder has low crystallinity and tends to amorphous structure. By grinding, 4-NP can be partially incorporated into the cavity of cyclodextrin to form inclusions. However, similarly, the high steric hindrance of the host molecule makes this inclusion lack regularity, and it is difficult to form a periodic regular structure.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Condition exploration and optimization of ASPT-β-CD toward 4-NP detection
ce:section.ce:para: Previous reports have demonstrated that polarity, pH, host-guest inclusion behavior etc. will greatly affect the fluorescence properties of hemicyanine dyes[35,53]. Therefore, optimizing the detection environment to amplify the fluorescence signal is crucial for the realization of dye probe functions. This work, we investigated the effects of polarity, pH and reaction time on the fluorescence signal. Firstly, three representative solvents with different polarities (methanol, DMF, and water) are used as probe detection environments. It can be concluded from Fig. 4A that the UV–visible absorption spectrum of ASPT-β-CD was red-shifted with the solvent polarity increases (orientational polarizability Δƒ: Methanol<DMF<Water), and in which case, the fluorescence excitation spectrum of ASPT-β-CD aqueous solution has a maximum overlap with the absorption of 4-NP, which is beneficial to the fluorescence quenching of the dye.

The stability of the probe ASPT-β-CD has certain requirements during the actual sample testing process to ensure the accuracy of the test. When the ASPT-β-CD power was exposed to the atmosphere for several months. Their aqueous solutions maintain strong optical stability and are not easily bleached and oxidized. The results from Fig. 4B show that the fluorescence intensity of ASPT-β-CD probe remains stable at 30 °C for at least three months. It can be seen that modification of cyclodextrin not only improves the pH stability but also enhances the structural stability. The good pH and structural stability of ASPT-β-CD are conducive to the optimal selection of pH and reaction time in the subsequent 4-NP detection environment.

The pH value and reaction time can influence the quenching efficiency of 4-NP. As shown in Fig. 4C, the pH of phosphate buffer solutions which varied from 2 to 11 was used to investigate the fluorescence intensity change of ASPT-β-CD. After the addition of 4-NP, the relative fluorescence intensities F0-F of ASPT-β-CD system first remained stable when pH is lower than 6. In the acidic environment, the reduction of F0 is mainly due to the fluorescence quenching caused by the host-guest interaction. And then F0-F increased with the pH increases, and reached the maximum value when pH = 8. In alkaline environment, the internal filtration effect and the host-guest interaction synergistically improved the fluorescence quenching efficiency. Therefore, we chose the optimal pH = 8 as the experimental condition. In addition, we also studied the quenching efficiency of 2-NP and 3-NP at different pH. As isomers of 4-NP, 2-NP and 3-NP have similar structures of 4-NP and are also easily encapsulated by cyclodextrins[54–56]. As Fig. 3C shows, absorption spectrum of 2-NP has a very low overlap with the excitation spectrum of ASPT-β-CD. And what is more, for 3-NP, this overlap can be ignored. So, the IFE effect between 2-NP, 3-NP and ASPT-β-CD can be ignored. Therefore, it is reasonable to suppose that the quenching of ASPT-β-CD mainly due to the host-guest interaction between ASPT-β-CD and nitrophenols (2-NP, 3-NP). This phenomenon is consistent with the fluorescence quenching of ASPT-β-CD in 4-NP acidic environment. The inclusion behavior between the nitrophenols and ASPT-β-CD helps to change the microenvironment of the luminophore ASPT connected to the β-CD molecule, thereby causing changes in its luminescence properties. As shown in Fig. 4C, 2-NP and 3-NP have certain quenching efficiency for ASPT-β-CD and are not sensitive to pH, which indicate that ASPT-β-CD have higher selectivity for 4-NP than other isomers in alkaline environment.

Fast fluorescence response is another important factor to achieve efficient fluorescent probes. Here, the effect of reaction time on the fluorescence intensity of ASPT-β-CD was investigated under the alkaline environment. As shown in Fig. 4D, the fluorescence intensity of ASPT-β-CD aqueous solution (pH = 8 of PBS) decreased rapidly within 2 min after the addition of 4-NP, and the fluorescence intensity decreased to one tenth of the original, reaching stability in 2 minutes. Therefore, 2 min is the shortest reaction time and indicates that ASPT-β-CD can achieve the sensitive detection of 4-NP in a short time.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fluorescence sensing of 4-NP based on ASPT-β-CD supramolecular fluorescent probe
ce:section.ce:para: Synergistic effect of IFE and host-guest interaction between ASPT-β-CD and 4-NP provides the possibility for 4-NP detection ( Fig. 5A). Fig. 5B shows the fluorescence spectra of ASPT-β-CD decreased with increasing concentration of 4-NP from 0 to 10 μM. A good linear relationship between F-F0 and 4-NP concentration in the range of 0.05–1.00 µM was observed in Fig. 5C, with the linear equation F-F0 = −80.125[4-NP]-19.737 (R2 = 0.9903), and the limit of detection (LOD, S/N = 3) of 4-NP was 0.01 µM. Compared to those of other analytical methods reported previously (Table S1), LOD of this work is at a comparable level or even lower. In addition, the value is far below the allowable limit 0.43 μM in drinking water given by the US Environmental Protection Agency (EPA)[32,57].

In order to evaluate the interference resistance of ASPT-β-CD system when detecting 4-NP, the possible interference has been examined. As shown in Fig. 5D, 4-NP, 2-NP, 3-NP, 4-AP, CC, RC, HQ, OPD, MPD, PPD, PHL, 4-CP, 4-BP, 4-FP, and phenol at 10 μM almost do not quench the fluorescence of the ASPT-β-CD (1 mM) while 4-NP (10 μM) quenched the fluorescence effectively, implying that the proposed method has good selectivity for detection of 4-NP. Moreover, as the pH of the sensing system increased from 6 to 8, significant fluorescence quenching was observed. When the pH of the sensing system reached 8, the difference in fluorescence intensity (F0-F) reached the maximum. the detection system still shows excellent reversibility after 5 consecutive cycles without remarkable fatigue (Fig. 5E)

The detection of 4-NP in real samples is important because 4-NP may cause pollution and harm to the environment when it enters water bodies. In order to evaluate the practical application of ASPT-β-CD, the standard addition method (1.0, 2.0 and 3.0) was used to analyze the 4-NP in the water samples of Nai River in Taian city. As shown in Table 1, the recoveries of the method were in the range of 98.0∼102.5% with the relative standard deviation (RSD) less than 3%, suggesting good accuracy and precision of the proposed method.

In summary, we designed and synthesized one β-CD modified hemicyanine fluorophore ASPT-β-CD for 4-NP detection. By integrating host-guest interaction and IFE, the proposed sensor ASPT-β-CD displayed significant "turn-off" after adding 4-NP under alkaline condition. Moreover, the detection system showed a fast response to 4-NP in 2 min and low LOD about 0.01 μM, and displayed good selectivity because of the host-guest inclusion between 4-NP and ASPT-β-CD. It is worth noting that this method has been successfully applied to the determination of 4-NP in lake water samples, indicating that the obtained ASPT-β-CD has great practical application potential in the environmental field.

Yanhui Wei: Writing – review & editing, Writing – original draft, Visualization, Validation, Supervision, Investigation, Data curation, Conceptualization. Fengyan Fang: Investigation, Formal analysis, Data curation. Zhaolei Zhang: Writing – original draft, Methodology, Investigation, Data curation, Conceptualization. Pingping Zhang: Formal analysis, Data curation. Hongchao Ma: Writing – original draft, Visualization, Validation, Supervision, Methodology, Funding acquisition, Data curation, Conceptualization. Xiaokang Zhang: Formal analysis, Data curation.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: We acknowledge the financial support from the National Natural Science Foundation of China (Project no. 22005180 and no. 21972167).

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data associated with this article can be found in the online version at doi:10.1016/j.colsurfa.2024.134087.
